## General Guide
- [Morimens New Players Handbook](<https://docs.google.com/document/d/1THjxR9hHs4NWHqSWHHa2dHAGuE_cRGelfzXdBfCnIlM/edit?usp=sharing>) by Cheri
- [Game/Realms/Stats Mechanics Guide](https://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1511362816362283118) by Mythag University
- [Store Guide](https://discordapp.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1442292634575175710) by Mythag University 
- [Morimens Database and Teambuiler](https://skeydb.com/#/database) by Fjant
## Mode Specific
- [P-Dive Shortest Map Analysis](<https://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1483756178340315216>) by Aurora
## Compendium
- [Morimens Compendium (unit builds and other info)](<https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1TCU7LJRzqKeuvLe97y_TfGZ-j6jq5bZX6hskAAJ2mFQ/edit?gid=167686509#gid=167686509>) by Mythag University

## Compendium

embeds.title: Morimens Covenants In A Nutshell
embeds.description:
In this video im explaining the Morimens Covenant System so Beginners can have a better understanding of it, together we are going over how to get covenants, how to upgrade them and how to start off as an early game player. Hope you enjoy!

-----------------------------------------------------------

- Take big damage ? white
- Take little damage, enemy vulnerable, corrosion applied : red
- None of the above ( most of the time ) : leave it blue

More accurately

Blue: Obtain a Tentacle at End Turn, also Default
White: Reduce Damage Taken, by mitigating it with a Shield
Red: Burst Turn, each Attack will be followed up by Tentacle /s

Oh, and I don't think I made it clear: E3 means base+3, OE means base+3+4, and AA is base+3+4+8, which means the max for an unit is 16. But, like said here, it's not necessary in 90% of the cases (not counting the fact that is fucking expensive)
